repo_prior_art.cite: eid_proximal_completeness_v1 angle3: finite Farkas bridge-realization frontier for latent negative-control feasibility; current null-cone version is narrower but close

published_prior_art.cite: BalkePearl1997 JASA: finite latent-variable sharp bounds via linear programming/Farkas-style feasibility; proximal null-cone specialization not covered

repo_prior_art.cite: eid_proximal_bridge_quotient_v1 only rejects the algebraic null-annihilator quotient as already solved; it does not give same-quotient different-latent-sharp-set laws

published_prior_art.cite: ZhangLiMiaoTchetgen2023 covers nonunique bridge functional invariance, not quotient-equivalent observed laws with different latent sharp ATE sets

repo_prior_art.cite: eid_proximal_phase_v1 Support Proposition 1: null-annihilator/full-rank proximal ATE point-identification reduction

published_prior_art.cite: MiaoGengTchetgen2018 and CuiPuShiMiaoTchetgen2024: standard full-rank/completeness proximal point-identification benchmark

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 1
novelty_flags.one_line: The headline promises computable M_a(P), b_a(P), Pi_a(P), and a Farkas certificate, but Exhibit 9.1 computes only T and an algebraic null vector, not the advertised certificate from primitives.

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 2
novelty_flags.one_line: The same-quotient/different-sharp-set witness is not exhibited: §9 lacks the second observed law, endpoint ATE computation, and separating Farkas multiplier.

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 1
novelty_flags.one_line: The checklist misses BalkePearl1997 JASA, the direct finite latent-response-type LP/sharp-bounds comparator for Farkas feasibility over latent laws.

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 1
novelty_flags.one_line: ZhangLiMiaoTchetgen2023 is anchored only to the Abstract for the null-annihilator comparison; the proposal needs the exact theorem/equation/condition being tightened.

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 2
novelty_flags.one_line: The flagship non-equivalence claim is positioned mainly against ZhangLiMiaoTchetgen2023 and a single finite witness, not against a two-comparator chain or generic open class.

soundness_flags.label: Conjecture 1
soundness_flags.one_line: The finite matrix certificate is stated over arbitrary finite latent U, but support sizes are not locked; without a support cap there is no single finite M_a(P), b_a(P), Pi_a(P).

soundness_flags.label: Formal setup
soundness_flags.one_line: The phrase 'induced bridges' of a latent law is undefined in the setup, which matters because bridge solutions are explicitly nonunique.

soundness_flags.label: Theorem 1
soundness_flags.one_line: The assumptions omit the latent exchangeability/assignment restriction needed to connect the unique observed bridge functional to the causal ATE sharp set.

soundness_flags.label: Conjecture 2
soundness_flags.one_line: The proof route says to solve two latent-completion systems but gives no concrete second law or algebraic obstruction beyond the partial 2-by-3 operator.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Lock a latent support cap K in §2/§6 and restate Conjecture 1 as a K-indexed finite certificate; then compute M, b, Pi on Exhibit 9.1.
Add Balke-Pearl 1997 as a comparator and explain exactly why proximal negative-control null-liftability is not just their response-type LP with renamed variables.
Replace the partial witness with two complete finite observed laws P and Q, including outcome regressions, latent completions for P, and a Farkas multiplier excluding Q.
If flagship remains the target, strengthen Conjecture 2 from a single witness to an open-class separation or add a second named comparator whose workflow would be changed by the certificate.

recommended_next_step: Revise: fix support/causal-assumption well-posedness, add the missing finite-LP comparator anchors, and compute the promised §9 certificate/witness before asking for flagship acceptance.
